component_libraries.name: Orion UI Kit
component_libraries.description: A responsive React-based UI component library and design system for internal dashboards and admin tools, including accessibility-first components, layout primitives, and theming.

component_libraries.name: Aurora Admin Components
component_libraries.description: A modular component set for building operations and admin consoles with dark-mode support and granular permission-aware UI patterns.

component_libraries.name: Nova Mobile UI
component_libraries.description: Mobile-first UI components optimized for responsive marketing and campaign microsites with touch-friendly controls.

component_libraries.name: Eclipse Legacy Widgets
component_libraries.description: A set of legacy jQuery-based widgets maintained for backward compatibility with older internal tools.

component_libraries.name: Zen Financial Dashboard Kit
component_libraries.description: Composable chart, table, and filter components focused on financial analytics dashboards with built-in localization support.

component_libraries.name: Quartz Vanilla Components
component_libraries.description: Lightweight framework-agnostic UI components implemented in vanilla JavaScript and web components for maximum portability.

component_libraries.name: Atlas Vue Design System
component_libraries.description: A comprehensive Vue-based UI design system with tokens, components, and patterns aligned to the corporate brand guidelines.

component_libraries.name: Meridian Angular Components
component_libraries.description: Enterprise-ready Angular components for building internal workflow and case management applications.

component_libraries.name: Plasma Email Templates
component_libraries.description: HTML and MJML-based component set for transactional and marketing emails with support for multiple brands.

component_libraries.name: Helix Internal Admin Theme
component_libraries.description: A proprietary theme and component set for internal-only administration tools, not intended for external open source use.

policies.title: Corporate Open Source Contribution Policy
policies.summary: Defines the standard process and requirements for contributing code, documentation, and other assets to external open source projects.
policies.content: This policy describes how employees may contribute to open source projects in an individual or corporate capacity. It covers contribution approval workflows, code review expectations, CLA and DCO requirements, security and licensing checks, and guidelines for interacting with external maintainers. All contributions must go through the internal review process outlined here before being submitted upstream.

policies.title: Frontend Projects Contribution Guidelines
policies.summary: Detailed contribution expectations for corporate-owned frontend repositories, including coding standards and UI accessibility requirements.
policies.content: These guidelines apply to all frontend and UI repositories maintained under the corporate GitHub organization. Contributors must follow the documented coding standards, linting and formatting rules, and accessibility checklists. Pull requests should include Storybook updates where applicable, visual regression test results, and links to any relevant design specifications. The document also explains how to label issues as good first issue to support new contributors.

policies.title: Data Platform Contribution Policy
policies.summary: Contribution policy for internal and external data platform and pipeline repositories.
policies.content: This policy sets the rules for contributing to the data platform ecosystem, including ingestion frameworks, transformation libraries, and analytics tooling. It outlines review responsibilities, data privacy considerations, dependency management practices, and how to document schema and contract changes. Contributions must be accompanied by migration plans and rollback procedures when applicable.

policies.title: Legacy Systems Contribution Policy
policies.summary: Describes contribution constraints for legacy and end-of-life systems that remain in maintenance mode.
policies.content: Contributions to legacy repositories focus on security fixes, critical bug corrections, and compliance requirements. New feature development is generally not allowed unless explicitly approved by the product steering group. This policy explains the reduced release cadence, test coverage expectations, and configuration freeze rules that apply to these systems.

policies.title: Open Source Security Policy
policies.summary: Defines security requirements for consuming and publishing open source software within the organization.
policies.content: The Open Source Security Policy mandates regular dependency scanning, vulnerability triage, and timely patching of critical issues. It requires the use of approved scanners, defines severity thresholds for remediation SLAs, and describes the escalation process for security advisories. The policy also documents how to handle coordinated disclosure with external communities.

policies.title: Default Project Governance Policy
policies.summary: Standard governance framework for new open source projects initiated by the company.
policies.content: This governance policy defines default roles (maintainers, contributors, reviewers), decision-making models, and conflict resolution processes for corporate open source projects. It provides recommendations on establishing a technical steering committee, documenting a code of conduct, and handling long-term maintenance and ownership transitions.

policies.title: Open Source Licensing Policy
policies.summary: Provides guidance on selecting, approving, and complying with open source licenses.
policies.content: This policy lists the set of pre-approved licenses such as MIT, Apache 2.0, and BSD-3-Clause, as well as licenses that require additional review. It explains copyleft implications, attribution requirements, and obligations around source code distribution. It also describes the internal review workflow for introducing new licenses into the approved set.

policies.title: Open Source Documentation Style Guide
policies.summary: Standardizes language, formatting, and structure for documentation across open source projects.
policies.content: The documentation style guide defines the preferred structure for README files, contribution guides, and API references. It specifies tone, inclusive language guidelines, terminology, and formatting rules for code samples and configuration snippets. Projects are encouraged to adopt this guide to reduce onboarding friction for contributors.

policies.title: Security Incident Response for Open Source Components
policies.summary: Outlines how the organization responds to security incidents involving open source dependencies or projects.
policies.content: This policy describes how to assess, triage, and respond to security incidents in open source components. It covers coordination with upstream maintainers, internal communication channels, temporary mitigations, and post-incident reviews. The document also defines responsibilities across security, engineering, and operations teams.

policies.title: Foundation Project Governance Addendum
policies.summary: Additional governance requirements for projects donated to or operated within external foundations.
policies.content: For projects managed under external open source foundations, this addendum explains how corporate governance aligns with foundation-specific rules. It addresses representation in technical steering committees, voting processes, budgeting considerations, and handling of trademarks and branding.

security_advisories.title: Critical RCE via Insecure Serialization in Orion UI Kit
security_advisories.description: A critical remote code execution vulnerability was identified in the server-side preview utility used by Orion UI Kit when processing untrusted serialized layout data. Attackers can craft a malicious payload that leads to arbitrary code execution in the preview environment.
security_advisories.remediation_guidance: Immediately disable the server-side preview feature or restrict it to trusted internal data. Upgrade to Orion UI Kit version 3.4.2 or later, which replaces the vulnerable serialization mechanism with a safe JSON-based format and adds strict input validation.

security_advisories.title: Critical Supply Chain Compromise in Internal NPM Registry
security_advisories.description: A critical incident was detected where a dependency used by multiple frontend projects was replaced with a malicious version in the internal NPM registry. The compromised package attempts to exfiltrate CI credentials and environment variables.
security_advisories.remediation_guidance: Rebuild all affected artifacts with the verified clean version of the dependency and rotate CI credentials. Block the malicious package version in the registry and implement additional signing and verification checks on all internal packages.

security_advisories.title: Critical Reflected XSS in Marketing Microsites Using Nova Mobile UI
security_advisories.description: Certain marketing microsites built with Nova Mobile UI reflect unsanitized query parameters into HTML, allowing attackers to inject arbitrary JavaScript. This vulnerability can be exploited via crafted links shared through email or social media.
security_advisories.remediation_guidance: Sanitize all user-controlled inputs before rendering them into HTML, and adopt the secure routing helpers shipped in Nova Mobile UI 2.1.0. Enable a strict Content Security Policy (CSP) on affected microsites to reduce the impact of potential XSS issues.

security_advisories.title: Critical CI Token Exposure in Atlas Vue Design System Pipelines
security_advisories.description: Continuous integration logs for Atlas Vue Design System were found to contain unredacted access tokens due to misconfigured logging settings, potentially allowing attackers with log access to impersonate the CI service.
security_advisories.remediation_guidance: Rotate all affected CI tokens immediately and scrub sensitive data from past logs where possible. Enable secrets masking in the CI configuration and add automated checks that fail builds if tokens are detected in log output.

security_advisories.title: High Privilege Escalation via Misconfigured Roles in Admin Dashboard
security_advisories.description: An authorization flaw in the Zen Financial Dashboard Kit integration allows read-only users to gain write access by manipulating role identifiers in API requests.
security_advisories.remediation_guidance: Apply server-side authorization checks based on authenticated user identity rather than client-supplied role identifiers. Update to Zen Financial Dashboard Kit 1.9.3, which includes hardened role validation utilities.

security_advisories.title: Medium CSRF Risk in Orion UI Settings Panel
security_advisories.description: The settings panel built with Orion UI Kit lacks CSRF protections on certain configuration endpoints, allowing attackers to trigger unwanted changes if a victim visits a malicious page while authenticated.
security_advisories.remediation_guidance: Enable CSRF protection on all state-changing endpoints, validate same-site cookies, and ensure that sensitive operations require explicit user confirmation where appropriate.

security_advisories.title: Low Information Disclosure via Verbose Error Logs
security_advisories.description: Verbose error logs in Eclipse Legacy Widgets may contain internal file paths and implementation details that could aid an attacker in reconnaissance.
security_advisories.remediation_guidance: Reduce log verbosity in production, scrub internal paths from error messages, and route detailed diagnostics to restricted log channels accessible only to administrators.

security_advisories.title: Critical Template Injection in Plasma Email Templates
security_advisories.description: User-supplied data is interpolated directly into email templates without proper escaping, enabling server-side template injection in certain deployment configurations.
security_advisories.remediation_guidance: Upgrade to the latest Plasma Email Templates release, which enables strict escaping by default and provides safe helper functions. Audit existing templates for unsafe dynamic expressions and replace them with parameterized variables.

security_advisories.title: High Session Fixation Risk Due to Missing SameSite Attributes
security_advisories.description: Several internal tools built on Meridian Angular Components issue session cookies without SameSite flags, increasing the risk of session fixation and CSRF-style attacks.
security_advisories.remediation_guidance: Set SameSite=Lax or SameSite=Strict on all session cookies and ensure secure and HttpOnly flags are enabled. Review authentication flows for potential compatibility issues before rollout.

security_advisories.title: Medium Open Redirect in Internal Auth Callback
security_advisories.description: An open redirect vulnerability in the authentication callback used by Helix Internal Admin Theme could allow attackers to redirect users to malicious domains after login.
security_advisories.remediation_guidance: Implement an allowlist of redirect URLs, validate callback parameters on the server, and avoid reflecting arbitrary query parameters into redirect destinations.

security_advisories.title: Low License Compliance Risk in Quartz Vanilla Components
security_advisories.description: A transitive dependency of Quartz Vanilla Components was found to use a copyleft license that may be incompatible with some downstream products.
security_advisories.remediation_guidance: Replace the affected dependency with a permissively licensed alternative or obtain an explicit legal exception where appropriate. Update the bill of materials (SBOM) to reflect the new dependency set.

security_advisories.title: High Risk from Insecure Default Configurations in Sample Projects
security_advisories.description: Sample applications packaged with several UI component libraries ship with insecure default configurations, including hard-coded test credentials and debug endpoints.
security_advisories.remediation_guidance: Remove hard-coded credentials, disable debug endpoints in production builds, and clearly mark sample configurations as development-only. Provide hardened configuration examples in documentation.

events.title: Open Source License Compliance Essentials Workshop
events.description: A hands-on workshop covering the fundamentals of open source license compliance, including common licenses, attribution requirements, and internal review workflows.

events.title: Open Source License Compliance Clinic (Q&A)
events.description: An interactive clinic focused on real-world license compliance scenarios. Bring your questions about MIT, Apache 2.0, GPL, and mixed-license projects.

events.title: Advanced Open Source License Compliance Workshop
events.description: Deep dive into complex licensing situations including dual licensing, AGPL, and SaaS-specific considerations.

events.title: Open Source License Compliance Foundations Training
events.description: Introductory training for engineers and product managers on identifying, documenting, and reviewing open source licenses.

events.title: Open Source Governance for Product Owners
events.description: A talk for product owners on setting up effective governance models for internal and external open source projects.

events.title: Inner Source Patterns Workshop
events.description: A collaborative workshop exploring patterns and anti-patterns when adopting inner source within large engineering organizations.

events.title: Community Building for Maintainers Meetup
events.description: Informal meetup for project maintainers to share experiences on onboarding contributors and managing healthy communities.

events.title: Security Best Practices in CI Pipelines Workshop
events.description: Hands-on session on integrating SCA, SAST, and secret scanning into CI pipelines for open source projects.

events.title: Responding to Open Source Security Advisories
events.description: Webinar on triaging and responding to security advisories like CVEs affecting corporate open source projects.

events.title: Corporate Open Source Summit 2026
events.description: Annual internal conference celebrating open source adoption, featuring keynotes, lightning talks, and strategy sessions.

events.title: Maintainers Roundtable: Inner Source vs Open Source
events.description: A facilitated roundtable for technical leads comparing inner source and open source collaboration models.

events.title: Open Source Governance Training for Engineering Leads
events.description: Training module for engineering leaders on governance models, decision-making, and contributor stewardship.

projects.name: Python Onboarding Helper
projects.description: A beginner-friendly toolkit that automates environment setup, project scaffolding, and first-PR checklists for new Python contributors in the organization. It includes templates, linters, and guided walkthroughs designed to make first contributions straightforward and low-risk.
projects.contribution_guide_content: Thank you for your interest in contributing to Python Onboarding Helper! This guide walks you through setting up your development environment, picking a first issue, and opening your first pull request. Start by reading the Getting Started section in the README, then choose an issue labeled good first issue or documentation. All changes must include tests where appropriate and must pass the automated CI checks. Please follow the code style enforced by our linters and run `make check` before submitting. See the Issue Lifecycle section below for details on triage, review expectations, and our code of conduct.

projects.name: ML Feature Store
projects.description: A Python-based feature store for managing machine learning features across teams. It supports batch and streaming ingestion, point-in-time joins, and integrations with popular model training pipelines.
projects.contribution_guide_content: Contributions to the ML Feature Store are welcome from data engineers, ML engineers, and analysts. Before opening a pull request, please create an issue describing your proposal and link any relevant design documents. All changes must include unit tests and, for storage adapters, integration tests. See the Architecture Overview section for guidance on extending core abstractions. We follow semantic versioning; breaking changes must be clearly documented and discussed in advance.

projects.name: Lightweight API Gateway
projects.description: A Python-powered lightweight API gateway providing routing, rate limiting, authentication hooks, and service discovery integration for internal microservices.
projects.contribution_guide_content: To contribute to Lightweight API Gateway, start by opening an issue describing the bug or enhancement you are proposing. For new features, please include an API sketch and rollout considerations. Run the full test suite with `pytest` and the lint checks with `make lint` before submitting. Security-sensitive changes, such as auth plugins, require review from the security team; see the Security Review section for details.

projects.name: Data Quality Toolkit
projects.description: A Python library for defining, executing, and reporting on data quality checks across batch and streaming data pipelines.
projects.contribution_guide_content: This project welcomes contributions ranging from new validation rules to connectors for additional data sources. Before coding, please search existing issues and open a new proposal if needed. Tests are mandatory for all new checks, and documentation should be updated with examples. See the Contributing New Checks section for guidance and code samples.

projects.name: Event Streamer
projects.description: A Python framework for building resilient event-driven data pipelines on top of Kafka and cloud messaging services.
projects.contribution_guide_content: Event Streamer uses an RFC process for major changes. For smaller fixes and enhancements, open an issue and link it from your pull request. Ensure that integration tests against the docker-compose environment pass locally. See the Contributor Workflow section for branch naming conventions, commit message guidelines, and our review SLA.

projects.name: Feature Flag Service
projects.description: A simple Python-based feature flag service with SDKs for multiple languages, enabling gradual rollouts and A/B testing.
projects.contribution_guide_content: We welcome contributions to SDKs, rollout strategies, and administrative tooling. Please coordinate breaking API changes with the owning team. All SDKs must maintain parity with the core API as described in the Protocol Specification section of this document.

projects.name: Open Source Dependency Security Scanner
projects.description: A Python tool that scans project dependencies for known vulnerabilities, license issues, and policy violations, integrating with CI pipelines.
projects.contribution_guide_content: Security-related contributions must follow the responsible disclosure guidelines in SECURITY.md. For non-sensitive improvements, open a standard pull request. New ecosystem integrations should include sample configurations and documentation updates in the Integrations section.

projects.name: Marketing Analytics Dashboard
projects.description: A TypeScript and React-based analytics dashboard that provides marketing teams with campaign, funnel, and attribution insights powered by the internal analytics platform.
projects.contribution_guide_content: Contributions from marketing and engineering collaborators are encouraged, especially around new visualizations and documentation. Look for issues labeled documentation and good first issue if you are new to the project. All UI changes must include Storybook updates to keep components well documented.

projects.name: Orion JS Web Framework
projects.description: A batteries-included JavaScript web framework optimized for building internal tools and dashboards, featuring routing, data fetching, and built-in authentication helpers.
projects.contribution_guide_content: Orion follows a release train model with scheduled minor releases. Before proposing a new core feature, please open a design RFC and gather feedback. Bug fixes and documentation improvements can be submitted directly via pull requests linked to an issue. See the Testing Requirements section for guidance on end-to-end test coverage.

projects.name: Aurora JS Web Framework
projects.description: A modular JavaScript web framework focused on performance and tree-shakeable bundles, ideal for high-traffic customer-facing applications.
projects.contribution_guide_content: Aurora is optimized for contributors comfortable with advanced JavaScript and build tooling. Please read the Architecture and Performance Guidelines sections before contributing core changes. Micro-optimizations should be justified with benchmarks included in your pull request.

projects.name: Photon React Component Library
projects.description: A TypeScript-based React component library implementing the corporate design system, including layout primitives, form controls, and data visualization components.
projects.contribution_guide_content: Contributions should align with the design tokens and accessibility standards defined in the Design Guidelines. All new components must include Storybook stories and accessibility notes. See the Component Definition Checklist section before opening a pull request.

projects.name: Observability Sidecar Agent
projects.description: A Go-based sidecar agent for collecting metrics, traces, and logs from containerized applications and forwarding them to centralized observability backends.
projects.contribution_guide_content: We accept contributions for new telemetry exporters, processors, and configuration options. Changes must maintain backward compatibility or be gated behind feature flags. Refer to the Contributor Setup section for instructions on running the test harness locally.

projects.name: License Compliance Automation
projects.description: A Java-based tool that automates license scanning, notice file generation, and policy checks for multi-language repositories.
projects.contribution_guide_content: Because this project is closely tied to legal requirements, changes to policy logic must be reviewed by the legal team. See the Policy Change Process section. Technical contributions such as performance improvements and new output formats follow the standard pull request workflow described in this guide.

projects.name: Design Tokens Sync Tool
projects.description: A JavaScript CLI and library for synchronizing design tokens between design tools and codebases, supporting multiple target platforms.
projects.contribution_guide_content: Contributors should coordinate with the design systems team before introducing new token categories. See the Supported Token Types section for current capabilities and the Roadmap section for planned work. All new exporters must include unit tests and at least one usage example.

issues.title: Add step-by-step guide for first-time contributors
issues.description: Create a new "First Contribution Walkthrough" section in the documentation that shows, step by step, how a new engineer can pick a good first issue, fork the repo, run tests locally, and open a pull request. Reuse existing README content where possible and include at least one screenshot or terminal example.

issues.title: Add pre-commit hook examples to templates
issues.description: Extend the project scaffolding templates to include optional pre-commit hook configurations (black, isort, flake8). Update the documentation to explain how to enable or disable these hooks for a new project.

issues.title: Refactor CLI argument parsing to use subcommands
issues.description: The current CLI uses a flat list of arguments that has become hard to extend. Refactor the CLI entrypoint to use subcommands (e.g., `init`, `check`, `report`) while maintaining backward compatibility for existing scripts.

issues.title: Document feature lineage best practices
issues.description: Add a dedicated documentation page that explains how to model feature lineage in the ML Feature Store, including examples for batch and streaming pipelines. Cover common pitfalls and how to keep lineage metadata in sync over time.

issues.title: Add Redis online store adapter
issues.description: Implement a new online store adapter backed by Redis, including connection management, basic health checks, and configuration options. Provide integration tests and update the storage backends section of the docs.

issues.title: Support per-API key rate limiting policies
issues.description: Extend the rate limiting middleware to support policies defined per API key, including separate burst and sustained limits. Update configuration schema and provide migration notes for existing deployments.

issues.title: Add examples for streaming data quality checks
issues.description: Create documentation and code examples that show how to run data quality checks on streaming data sources (e.g., Kafka). Include a minimal end-to-end example pipeline and guidance on alerting.

issues.title: Fix consumer group rebalancing bug under high load
issues.description: Under heavy load, consumers occasionally miss commit offsets during group rebalances. Investigate the root cause and implement a fix to ensure at-least-once delivery guarantees are preserved.

issues.title: Improve documentation for percentage rollouts
issues.description: The current docs for percentage-based rollouts are confusing for non-technical stakeholders. Rewrite the section with diagrams and concrete examples that explain how users are bucketed and how rollouts can be safely ramped up.

issues.title: Add GitLab CI integration example
issues.description: Provide a documented example of integrating the scanner with GitLab CI, including a template `.gitlab-ci.yml` file, recommended stages, and failure conditions based on severity thresholds.

issues.title: Clarify UTM parameter mapping in documentation
issues.description: Update the documentation for the data model to clearly describe how UTM parameters are mapped into dimensions used by the dashboard. Include a table of example URLs and the resulting fields, targeting marketing users who are not familiar with the underlying schema.

issues.title: Fix inconsistent tooltip formatting on funnel chart
issues.description: Tooltips on the funnel chart sometimes display percentages with too many decimal places and inconsistent currency formats. Standardize tooltip formatting and add unit tests for the formatter utilities.

issues.title: Add support for multi-touch attribution model
issues.description: Extend the attribution model options to include a multi-touch model with configurable decay. This will require backend API changes and new UI controls on the settings page.

issues.title: Security: Add CSRF protection helpers for default auth flow
issues.description: Provide built-in CSRF protection helpers that can be easily wired into the default authentication flow, including middleware and documentation. Align with internal security guidelines.

issues.title: Add streaming SSR support
issues.description: Implement experimental support for streaming server-side rendering to improve perceived performance on slow connections. Include feature flag controls and benchmarks.

issues.title: Security: Review and document default CSP headers
issues.description: Audit the default Content-Security-Policy headers recommended in the Aurora documentation and ensure they align with current security best practices. Update the docs with examples for common deployment scenarios.

issues.title: Improve tree-shaking guide with real-world examples
issues.description: The current tree-shaking documentation is theoretical. Add real-world examples demonstrating how to configure bundlers (Webpack, Rollup, Vite) to minimize Aurora bundle size.

issues.title: Add date range picker component
issues.description: Design and implement a date range picker component consistent with the existing date input styles. Include accessibility support for keyboard navigation and screen readers, plus Storybook stories.

issues.title: Document configuration for multi-cluster deployments
issues.description: Add a documentation section describing best practices for deploying the Observability Sidecar Agent in multi-cluster Kubernetes environments, including label strategies and resource limits.

issues.title: Support SPDX tag parsing in license scanner
issues.description: Enhance the license scanner to parse SPDX license identifiers from source headers and package manifests, improving accuracy and reducing false positives.

issues.title: Add support for Figma variables-based token export
issues.description: Figma is rolling out variables as the preferred way to manage design tokens. Add an exporter that pulls tokens from Figma variables and maps them into the existing token schema.
